_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed'; use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Establish a real release process so consumers pin a version instead of @master (issue #23). Adds CHANGELOG.md (keep-a-changelog) whose 0.2.0 entry summarizes the current gate state accumulated since the floating v0 tag (proofgate→Plumbline rename, ESCALATE→REVIEW / REVISE→REWORK verdict rename, per-PR receipts, evidence-integrity poll-wait, propose/archive, receipt --write/--check, attempt history, tunable strictness, self-hosting, and the protected-paths migration-guard convention). Adds .github/workflows/release.yml which, on a pushed v*.*.* tag, type-checks + builds + tests, verifies the tag matches package.json version, extracts the matching CHANGELOG section as release notes, creates the GitHub Release, and conditionally attaches+checksums a dist tarball IF a built dist/ is present (packaging is owned by sibling PR #24, so artifact handling is optional and the release degrades to source-only). Adds RELEASING.md documenting how to cut v0.2.0 and how to move a major tag like v1. Updates README with a 'Pinning a version' section recommending a released tag (@v1) over @master and naming the unblocked consumers (managed-platform, 2.0, protocol, cuspr). Bumps package.json version 0.1.0→0.2.0 (version field ONLY — build scripts/dist are PR #24's).

validation_plan.reason: No source (.ts) changed, but the build must still type-check clean on this branch — proves the release workflow's build/test steps (tsc --noEmit, npm run build, npm test) will pass when the tag is cut.
Full suite must stay green: this change touches no gate logic, so all 101 tests must pass unchanged, confirming CHANGELOG/README/workflow/version edits are behavior-neutral.

validation_plan.command: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))" && awk -v ver=0.2.0 '$0 ~ "^## \\[" ver "\\]" {p=1; next} p && /^## \[/ {exit} p {print}' CHANGELOG.md
validation_plan.reason: The release workflow is only exercised on a real tag push (out of PR CI scope), so validate it statically: YAML parses, and the CHANGELOG section-extraction that produces the release notes returns the 0.2.0 body.

execution_evidence.command: python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml'))" && awk -v ver=0.2.0 '$0 ~ "^## \\[" ver "\\]" {p=1; next} p && /^## \[/ {exit} p {print}' CHANGELOG.md
execution_evidence.output_ref: YAML_OK; awk extracted the 69-line 0.2.0 changelog section for release notes

result_summary: Adds a release process: CHANGELOG.md (keep-a-changelog) with a 0.2.0 entry summarizing the accumulated gate state since v0; .github/workflows/release.yml that on a pushed v*.*.* tag builds/tests, verifies tag==package.json version, publishes a GitHub Release with CHANGELOG-derived notes, and conditionally attaches a checksummed dist tarball (optional — dist/packaging is sibling PR #24's, release degrades to source-only); RELEASING.md documenting how to cut v0.2.0 and move a major tag like v1; a README 'Pinning a version' section recommending @v1 over @master; and a package.json version bump 0.1.0→0.2.0 (version field only). Verified with tsc --noEmit (clean) and the full 101-test suite (all green), plus static validation of the workflow (YAML parses; the CHANGELOG section-extraction returns the 0.2.0 notes). Scope respected: no gate logic, review.ts, action bundling, build scripts, or AGENTS.md touched. self_modifying: true — the new .github/workflows/release.yml matches the protected .github/workflows/** glob, so this self-gates to REVIEW for human sign-off; the first tag/Release is human-triggered, not cut here.
